inputs.label: Surface Area
inputs.businessContext: Total surface area of the object/pipe exposed to ambient. Based on ISO 12241:2008 for thermal insulation.

inputs.label: Surface Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of the outer surface of the insulation or object. Required for convective and radiative heat loss calculation.

inputs.label: Ambient Temperature
inputs.businessContext: Temperature of surrounding air. Standard ambient conditions per ISO 6946.

inputs.label: Wind Speed
inputs.businessContext: Air velocity over surface. Affects convective heat transfer coefficient. Based on ASHRAE Handbook.

inputs.label: Emissivity
inputs.businessContext: Surface emissivity for radiative heat transfer. Typical values: oxidized steel 0.8, polished metal 0.1. Based on ASTM C1371.

inputs.label: Insulation Thickness
inputs.businessContext: Thickness of insulation layer. Used to compute thermal resistance. Based on ISO 12241.

inputs.label: Insulation Thermal Conductivity
inputs.businessContext: Thermal conductivity of insulation material. Typical: mineral wool 0.04, polyurethane 0.025. Based on ASTM C518.

inputs.label: Operation Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Annual operating hours. Used to calculate annual energy loss. Standard industrial assumption.

inputs.label: Energy Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost of energy per kWh. Used to calculate financial loss. Based on local utility rates.

formulas.heatLossWithInsulation: if insulationThickness > 0 then (surfaceTemperature - ambientTemperature) / (1/totalHeatTransferCoefficient + thermalResistanceInsulation) else heatLossPerArea

hiddenLossDrivers: if heatLossPerArea > 1000 then 'High heat loss per area'
suggestedActions: Use insulation with lower thermal conductivity
